LanguageData.WVC_XaG_GeneShapeshifter_Desc: Allows to change the pawn's xenotype to any desired one, provided there is sufficient genetic compatibility or genetic materials.
LanguageData.WVC_XaG_GeneShapeshifter_DisabledGenesRe: This person's genetic material is regrowing.
LanguageData.WVC_XaG_GeneShapeshifter_MinMatchPercent: The genetic match to the selected xenotype is too low. Minimum match should be {0}%.
LanguageData.WVC_XaG_ShapeshifterDialog_XenotypeGenes: Expected number of xenotype genes. This does not reflect the exact number of genes in the xenotype.
LanguageData.WVC_XaG_GeneticMaterial_GenesDesc: The total genetic material of all selected genes. For implantation, it should not exceed the genetic material collected by gene.
LanguageData.WVC_XaG_GeneticMaterial_ShifterDesc: Genetic material collected by the shapeshifter gene.
LanguageData.WVC_XaG_DialogEditShiftGenes_PawnHasGene: The pawn already has one of the selected genes.
LanguageData.WVC_XaG_DialogEditShiftGenes_Explanation: Genes can be implanted at the cost of genetic material. Implanted genes remove genes with which they conflict.
LanguageData.WVC_XaG_DialogEditShiftGenes_ConflictWit: The implantation will remove some of the pawn's genes.
LanguageData.WVC_XaG_DialogEditShiftGenes_Warning: Shapeshifter cannot remove genes individually. Implanted genes cannot be removed until the next shapeshift.
LanguageData.WVC_XaG_DialogEditShiftGenes_ChangeTypeW: This action will change the type of shapeshifter gene, if it was xenogene (xenogerm), it will become endogene (germline) and vice versa. The accumulated genetic material will be transferred.\n\nNote that genes implanted at the cost of genetic material are always implanted where the shapeshifter gene is located.
LanguageData.WVC_XaG_DialogEditShiftGenes_ChangeTypeM: Shapeshifter changed it's genetic type successfully.
LanguageData.WVC_XaG_ShapeshifterDialog_TrueFormDesc: This xenotype is one of the natural forms of the shapeshifter. It can be implanted regardless of the owner's genetic condition.
LanguageData.WVC_XaG_GeneRemover_Desc: Allows to remove any genes from the current geneset. Genes are removed one at a time.
LanguageData.WVC_XaG_GeneRemover_Warning: This action will result in the gene being removed from the pawn's geneset, which is irreversible. If the shapeshifter gene is present, this may provide genetic material or, on the contrary, consume it. The amount of genetic material is calculated relative to the complexity and archites of the gene.
LanguageData.WVC_XaG_ShapeshifterGizmoTip: <color=#8a8ae6>Shapeshifter</color> allows to change the pawn's xenotype at any time, this is limited only by the <color=#6bb6bb>genes regrowing</color> hediff. After <color=#8a8ae6>shapeshifting</color>, the pawn is guaranteed to receive <color=#6bb6bb>genes regrowing</color>, the duration of which depends on the complexity, metabolism and archites of the selected xenotype. If the pawn had <color=#6bb6bb>genes regrowing</color> at the time of shapeshifting, it is summed up.\n\n - <color=#8076ff>Genetic material</color>: {0}
LanguageData.WVC_XaG_ShapeshifterGenesImplant_Desc:
Allows the implantation of special genes using excess <color=#8076ff>genetic material</color>.

<color=#8076ff>Genetic material</color> accumulates when using shapeshift, each xenotype has its own amount of accumulated material, except for <color=#8a8ae6>true forms</color>, they always have a zero value.

Some sub-genes can also accumulate it.
LanguageData.WVC_XaG_FleshshaperGizmoTip:
Fleshshaper allows to change a pawn's <color=#8a8ae6>xenotype</color> to any of the <color=#8a8ae6>fleshmass xenotypes</color>, as well as <color=#8a8ae6>edit xenogenes</color>. Available <color=#8a8ae6>xenogenes</color> are determined relative to <color=#8a8ae6>xenotypes</color>. The list of available xenotypes can be expanded using <color=#8a8ae6>sub-genes</color>.

Changing a pawn's xenotype (<color=#8a8ae6>shapeshifting</color>) and editing its <color=#8a8ae6>xenogenes</color> triggers <color=#8a8ae6>gene regrowing</color>, which limits shapeshifting. The <color=#8a8ae6>duration</color> of gene regrowing depends on the biostats of the <color=#8a8ae6>implanted</color> genes; the more expensive the genes, the longer the debuff.

Xenogermline (<color=#8a8ae6>xenogenes</color>) will slowly lose <color=#8a8ae6>random gene</color> every day. This process cannot be stopped, but it rewards them with <color=#8a8ae6>genetic material</color> that can be spent to make certain genes permanent (ignored). This also increases <color=#8a8ae6>genetic match</color> with <color=#8a8ae6>xenotypes</color>, opening up more opportunities for shapeshifting.

 - <color=#8076ff>Genetic material</color>: {0}
LanguageData.WVC_XaG_Fleshshaper_XenogenesEditorTip:
Allows editing <color=#8a8ae6>xenogenes</color> within certain biostat limits. The complexity and archite limit depends on the total complexity and archite of endogenes; the metabolism limit is always a fixed value.

Most genes implanted this way will be <color=#8a8ae6>lost over time</color>. However, using <color=#8a8ae6>genetic material</color>, genes can be made permanent.

Genes that will be lost over time have a small <color=#8a8ae6>lock icon</color> in the upper right corner. Genes without an icon will not be gradually removed, but shapeshifting or other gene manipulation may still remove them. However, they can be safely re-implanted.
LanguageData.WVC_XaG_FaultyShapeDisease: {0} lost the {1} gene due to genetic overload.
LanguageData.WVC_XaG_EnergyshaperGizmoTip: Energyshifter allows to change a pawn's <color=#8a8ae6>xenotype</color> at almost any time. The starting set of xenotypes is limited to <color=#8a8ae6>metalkin</color> xenotypes; more xenotypes can be unlocked with subgenes. Each <color=#8a8ae6>shapeshift</color> (xenotype change) triggers <color=#8a8ae6>genes regrowing</color> and consumes all of the pawn's <color=#8a8ae6>nutrients</color>.\n\nA gene <color=#8a8ae6>passively</color> consumes nutrients every day, at a rate of {0}%, regardless of the total. Consumption can be modified by subgenes. If a pawn's <color=#8a8ae6>food need</color> is disabled, the gene cannot be used.
LanguageData.WVC_XaG_GeneEnergyshifter_BarTip:
Displays the <color=#8a8ae6>genetic energy</color> reserve in sync with the pawn's food requirement. If the entire battery is filled with yellow, it means the genetic match with all available xenotypes is 100%.

It also allows to adjust energy consumption by disabling/enabling subgenes.

 - <color=#8076ff>Nutrients consumption</color>: {0}%/day
